item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are too many details left out for me. What's really going on in the body? So, is local gravity changed? That is, if the woman stands, do the fauna fall out of trees? Does it rain? How is there room for clouds? How big are the trees? Microscopic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
including links to the author's other works on the page itself like that feels like part of an escalation that I'd really rather not encourage or see

Agreed completely. I wouldn't want to see a rules change or anything at all like that addressing stuff like this, but it's an additional strain on immersion for me as a reader and I find it tacky as hell. Pimp your stuff in the comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel really weird about the list of author's works at the bottom of the page.

Like, I get that the bottom of the page often contains non-diegetic elements (aside: diegetic is my new favorite word, can you tell), but including links to the author's other works on the page itself like that feels like part of an escalation that I'd really rather not encourage or see.

item.title: Re: heyoooooo
item.content:encoded: EDIT: Reverted the change I made, as it was against site rules. Soz!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"See Case File 3224-308 for details of this process with twins."

Unsettling indeed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmmm, I like the premise but I feel like it doesn't really go all the way with how out there the idea is. While I can appreciate brevity, perhaps the attention might have been better focused on the interior of the rainforest rather than the process of spreading through the body. Make it so you couldn't take out the rainforest and add in say, a pipe nightmare style sewer system and basically still have the same article, make it more unique. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus Christ, how horrifying.

This is probably the first contest entry so far that has actually manged to really creep me out, in particular the banana tree pulling a chest burster from Alien. You also get some props for having a particularly creative solution to the "Tropical" prompt.

Well done Rimple. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well this is quite horrible and inexplicable. It's a bit on the random side, but I like it. +1 for sheer creativity.

Three notes on semi-science though:
-The miniature rainforest described seems to be specifically be a tropical rainforest, so it would seem salient to explicitly specify that in the text.
-"This complication occurs in 0.5-4% of ectopic pregnancies" and "The translates to 12-23 pregnancies per year" don't seem to match. In the former, the maximum is eight times the minimum, in the latter, less than twice.
-In addition, the percentage is several orders of magnitude too high to result in less than two dozen cases per year. Sources aren't quite consistent with incidence rate for ectopic pregnancies, but most state one over 1%, so we'll go with that. There's 211 million pregnancies a year (source: http://www.who.int/whr/2005/chapter3/en/index3.html ), and 0.5% of 1% of that would result in about ten thousand cases of SCP-3224 a year.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yup!

SCP-3224 is considered anomalously non-fatal, as mortality rates are in excess of 70% in all mundane complications which cause comparable degrees of strain on a subject.

That applies to both parent and child, though I took it out when I was removing gendered language (originally that read "comparable degrees of strain on the mother and child"). I may re-edit it for clarity after the competition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the fetus surviving an ectopic pregnancy meant to be part of the anomaly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gah.

Gives new meaning to Jungle Boogie I guess

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kids in trunks.
Kids in trunks.
Taking over.
But it won't be long.

Or actually, it will be, because a banana tree just straight-up explodes out of your body like a fucking xenomorph and what the fUCK RIMPLE, I LOVED YOU

+1

item.title: heyoooooo
item.content:encoded:
Written for Day 2 of the 72 Hour Jam Contest, for the theme Tropical. Let me know if you feel I've hit that. And if anyone finds an image they feel would fit this, PM me plz.

Could not have done this without medical knowledge from TheGreatSea] and advice from PeppersGhost.

While you're here, may as well also check out my entry for day one, SCP-3772. This one was for the theme Murder Mystery.
